title: calea caracasana
intro: Calea caracasana, also known as the "Mexican Damiana," is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The medicinal properties of Calea caracasana include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of using Calea caracasana include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as improved digestion and immune function. Calea caracasana can be consumed as an herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, often combined with other herbs like valerian root or passionflower for enhanced sleep and relaxation benefits.
